source_jobs.title: Solutions Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Retire the ERP.

Light exists to replace factory-era ERPs with software that feels alive. Our Smart Financial Platform gives modern, global companies superpowers—automated accounting, real-time reporting, and financial flows that move at the speed of the business.

We build with our customers, ship fast, and obsess over craft. In a short time, Light has gone from idea to the operating core for leading companies like Lovable, Legora, and Keyshot. People don’t just use Light—they enjoy it.

We’re an early team defining a new software category. Think engineers who love debits and credits, designers who care about reconciliation states, and operators who treat finance as a product. If you’re excited to modernize how the world runs money—one workflow at a time—you’re in the right place.

Backed by world-class investors and advised by industry titans, we’re building category-defining products with the freedom to ship ambitiously and own outcomes.
Come help us make Light the global default for next-gen finance.


This Solutions Engineer role

 - Leading technical pre-sales activities for multinational clients evaluating Light’s platform, especially firms replacing or augmenting legacy ERPs.

 - Running product & technical demos that showcase how Light addresses complex finance workflows, system integrations, and scale.

 - Deep collaboration with prospects’ technical and finance stakeholders to understand current systems, data flows, and pain points.

 - Designing and proposing architecture & implementation plans that map to client needs, ensuring feasibility and scalability.

 - Supporting RFPs and responding to technical questions, clarifying how existing or custom integrations will work.

How you fit into the team

Your main mission is to translate complex customer requirements into compelling technical solutions — helping both clients and Light see how the platform delivers. You will act as a bridge between sales, product, engineering, implementation and customers. You’ll need strong presentation skills, deep ERP domain knowledge, and the ability to simplify complexity. This is a highly visible, client-facing role with direct responsibility for helping close deals and ensuring technical alignment early.


Your qualifications:

 - Bachelor’s degree in Accounting or equivalent experience.

 - 3+ years’ experience as a Solutions Engineer/Pre-Sales Engineer in an ERP/FMS context (vendor-side preferred).

 - Deep understanding of ERP implementation projects: data migrations, integrations, customisations, modules (finance, supply chain, etc.).

 - Excellent technical demo and presentation skills: able to clearly articulate system architecture, APIs, integration points, and trade-offs.

 - Strong relationship building skills: able to engage with finance leaders.

 - Hands-on experience with at least one major ERP system (e.g. NetSuite, Oracle, SAP) and familiarity with cloud-based systems.

 - Able to think strategically and practically: balancing customer requirements, technical constraints, and Light’s product roadmap.

 - Practical experience using AI or emerging technology tools to improve productivity, insight, or decision-making, with the ability to apply first-principles thinking to new problems.
   

Bonus points

 - Experience in SaaS / FinTech industry, especially working with multi-entity financial operations or global accounting standards.

 - Prior experience migrating customers from legacy ERPs to newer cloud financial platforms.

 - Knowledge of SQL or Python.

 - Experience with data transformation, ETL, or data modelling.

 - Familiarity with API-based integrations or middleware tools.

 - Multi-region implementation experience (e.g. EMEA + US) or working across different regulatory / tax environments.



A few tips to stand out

 - Share examples of complex technical pre-sales / solution engineering projects you’ve led.

 - Demonstrate how you think about integrations, data migrations, scalability, and managing technic

source_jobs.title: Business Development Representative
source_jobs.description:
🚀 BDR | HELP BUILD THE FUTURE OF FINANCE




RETIRE THE ERP.

We're building the financial platform replacing legacy ERPs.

Light automates accounting, reporting and financial operations for modern global businesses, giving finance teams real-time visibility without the chaos of disconnected systems.

Already trusted by companies like Lovable, Legora and Keyshot, we're growing fast and building an entirely new category of software. Backed by world-class investors, we're looking for ambitious people who want to build something that matters.




THE ROLE

This isn't a "book meetings forever" BDR role.

You'll own outbound, generate pipeline, partner closely with Account Executives and gain exposure to the full sales cycle. We want to develop future AEs, not career BDRs.

You'll be:

 - 🎯 Prospecting through phone, email and LinkedIn

 - 📞 Speaking with Finance Leaders, CFOs and C-suite executives

 - 🤝 Booking high-quality meetings that convert into revenue

 - 💡 Creating personalised outreach that actually gets replies

 - 🚀 Working alongside experienced AEs and learning how complex deals are won

 - 🤖 Using AI and modern sales tools to work smarter, not harder




WE'RE LOOKING FOR

You might have:

 - 1+ year as a SaaS BDR or 2-3 years in a high-performing outbound sales role

 - A track record of smashing targets and building pipeline

 - Confidence on the phone (you actually enjoy cold calling)

 - A curious, resilient, competitive mindset

 - A genuine interest in startups, technology and AI

Bonus points if you've sold into finance teams, fintech, ERP software or if you're already using AI to make yourself more productive.




WHY LIGHT?

✨ Competitive salary + equity opportunity

📈 Fast progression into an AE role

🌍 Remote-first with regular company off-sites

🏖 Generous holiday allowance

👶 Paid parental leave

🚀 Join one of Europe's fastest-growing AI startups while we're still early.




WANT TO STAND OUT?

Don't just send a CV.

Show us numbers. Show us impact. Tell us why Light, not just why "a startup." Side projects, AI experiments, entrepreneurial ideas...we love builders.

If you're excited by big challenges, ownership and helping redefine how finance works, we'd love to hear from you.



Come and help us replace the ERP.

source_jobs.title: Account Executive
source_jobs.description:
Retire the ERP.

Light exists to replace factory-era ERPs with software that feels alive. Our Smart Financial Platform gives modern, global companies superpowers—automated accounting, real-time reporting, and financial flows that move at the speed of the business.

We build with our customers, ship fast, and obsess over craft. In a short time, Light has gone from idea to the operating core for leading companies like Lovable, Legora, and Keyshot. People don’t just use Light—they enjoy it.

We’re an early team defining a new software category. Think engineers who love debits and credits, designers who care about reconciliation states, and operators who treat finance as a product. If you’re excited to modernize how the world runs money—one workflow at a time—you’re in the right place.

Backed by world-class investors and advised by industry titans, we’re building category-defining products with the freedom to ship ambitiously and own outcomes.
Come help us make Light the global default for next-gen finance.



The Account Executive role:

 - Leading sales cycles for Light as an SFP, managing deals from $30K to $200K+ and consistently hitting revenue targets.

 - Building relationships with C-suite executives and key decision makers, understanding their business needs and showing how our SFP solution helps.

 - Creating account plans and working closely with technical teams to develop solutions for target industries like asset-light, multinational companies.

 - Running effective customer meetings and negotiations, handling complex deals from first contact through contract signing.

 - Owning the AE to BDR relationship to generate pipeline as a team.


How you fit into the team:

Your main mission is to help prospective customers see the light. Aside from being based in-office in London or New York, this role requires you to be hungry, have an understanding of finance tools and knowledge of sales. We have a clear ICP defined with a leadlist.



This is a GTM role with the responsibility of sales and closing pipeline. You'll work with a small and stellar team with a deep understanding of the market who has done this before.


Your qualifications:

 - 3-6 years of closing experience and acing pipeline - preferably within ERPs.

 - Your Personality: Your exceptional interpersonal skills, energy and sense of ownership make you stand out.

 - Thriving in a Fast-Paced Environment: You thrive in a fast-paced, high-growth, and rapidly changing environment.

 - Attention to Detail: Your attention to detail, outstanding communication skills (phone, listening, and writing), set you up for success.

 - Self-Driven: Self-driven and ambitious, you have the desire to grow with Light.

 - Entrepreneurial Spirit: Your entrepreneurial spirit and experience with a strong growth mindset align perfectly.

 - Comfort in Quota-Carrying Environment: You are ambitious, self-motivated, and comfortable working in a quota-carrying environment.

 - Practical experience using AI or emerging technology tools to improve productivity, insight, or decision-making, with the ability to apply first-principles thinking to new problems.


Bonus points:

 - You're CPA and have a few years of experience within Big4, accounting or the CFO suite.

 - Sales Tools Familiarity: You have prior experience using Sales Automation/Prospecting Tools (e.g., Amplemarket, Salesloft, Outreach) and CRM (Hubspot).

 - Startup Experience: You've worked at a fast-growing startup.

 - Cold Calling Pro: You ace cold calling and relationship building. Wanna really get on our radar? Find our number, call and pitch us.


A few tips to stand out

 - Show us who you are through your application and don't limit yourself to a resume. Try not to be generic.

 - Have numbers to show so you can measure impact? Even better!

 - Most people can say they wanna work in a startup, but why this one? What are your expectations and what do you specifically wanna learn from us?


The good stuff

In addition to

source_jobs.title: AI Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Retire the ERP.

Light exists to replace factory-era ERPs with software that feels alive. Our Smart Financial Platform gives modern, global companies superpowers—automated accounting, real-time reporting, and financial flows that move at the speed of the business.

We build with our customers, ship fast, and obsess over craft. In a short time, Light has gone from idea to the operating core for leading companies like Lovable, Legora, and Keyshot. People don’t just use Light—they enjoy it.

We’re an early team defining a new software category. Think engineers who love debits and credits, designers who care about reconciliation states, and operators who treat finance as a product. If you’re excited to modernize how the world runs money—one workflow at a time—you’re in the right place.

Backed by world-class investors and advised by industry titans, we’re building category-defining products with the freedom to ship ambitiously and own outcomes.
Come help us make Light the global default for next-gen finance.



This AI Engineer role

We're looking for builders who can take a customer problem from idea to production, wherever in the stack it lives. At Light, AI writes code and the bottleneck has shifted from writing to thinking, so we want engineers who work end-to-end and ship things that matter.

We are primarily hiring at mid-level for this role. Junior candidates who are exceptional and genuinely AI-native are welcome. Senior candidates will be considered by exception; we have strong technical leadership and the bar is high.



Your qualifications:

2-6 years of experience ideally, plus...

 - You build things. Not prototypes, not demos. Working software in production, doing something real for real users. This is the non-negotiable.

 - You're genuinely AI-native. You've changed the way you work because of AI tooling, not just added it to your workflow. You have an instinct for where it helps, where it misleads, and how to use it without losing ownership of the outcome.

 - You've built with LLMs. You have hands-on experience building products or features on top of large language models. Agents are a bonus, but working knowledge of how LLMs behave in production is a must.

 - You're comfortable across the stack. You don't need to be a specialist in everything, but you can't have a lane you refuse to leave. Our stack is Kotlin and React. If you're strong in one and curious about the rest, that works. If you need someone else to handle the other side of the feature, it doesn't.

 - You care about the product. You think about the person on the other end. You notice when something feels clunky, even if it technically works. You have opinions about what good software feels like and you act on them.

 - You ship early and often. Small batches. Learn. Go again. You're not precious about your work. You'd rather put something real in front of a customer than spend another week perfecting it in isolation.
   

Bonus points:

 - Experience in fintech or systems where data integrity is non-negotiable

 - Familiarity with accounting concepts: debits, credits, reconciliation

 - Background in high-growth startups where everything was still being figured out



Our Tech Stack:

We build on AWS, with a Kotlin backend, a React frontend, and infrastructure managed in Terraform. It's a real production system that global finance teams depend on every day.
You don't need to have used every piece of it. You do need to be comfortable in this kind of environment: real scale, real customers, real stakes.



The good stuff

In addition to being part of a great team and working in a really fun and innovative environment, we offer:

💸 A competitive salary + potential stock options
🏝 25 days of annual leave + public holidays in the country where you are based.
🥳 Regular socials and company off-sites
🚀 A huge opportunity for career growth as you'll help shape a market-defining product.



The famous last words

Working at Light means you're embarking on an

source_jobs.title: Bank Connectivity Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Retire the ERP.

Light exists to replace factory-era ERPs with software that feels alive. Our Smart Financial Platform gives modern, global companies superpowers—automated accounting, real-time reporting, and financial flows that move at the speed of the business.

We build with our customers, ship fast, and obsess over craft. In a short time, Light has gone from idea to the operating core for leading companies like Lovable, Legora, and Keyshot. People don’t just use Light—they enjoy it.

We’re an early team defining a new software category. Think engineers who love debits and credits, designers who care about reconciliation states, and operators who treat finance as a product. If you’re excited to modernize how the world runs money—one workflow at a time—you’re in the right place.

Backed by world-class investors and advised by industry titans, we’re building category-defining products with the freedom to ship ambitiously and own outcomes.
Come help us make Light the global default for next-gen finance.



How you would fit into the team

This role sits at the heart of Light’s onboarding and engineering team. You’ll work closely with our clients, partners, banks and fellow engineers to build the money backbone of the Smart Financial Platform. Payments are a core pillar of Light’s mission — powering everything from cross-border transactions to automated accounting — and your work will directly shape how global businesses move money through our system.

You’ll set up and maintain host-to-host integrations (mainly over SFTP and SWIFT-via-SFTP), exchange SSH and PGP keys, run payment and statement tests, and keep our payment pipelines healthy.

It’s not pure software engineering, but you’ll read and write code when needed, debugging payment formats, checking database records, or improving an integration. If you like combining structured ops work with technical problem-solving, this role is for you.

You’ll work in the Integration Delivery team, partnering with bank tech teams, customer success, and our engineers.

The Bank Connectivity Engineer role

 - Set up and maintain secure host-to-host bank connections (SFTP), including SSH/PGP key exchange and endpoint configuration.

 - Integrate and test payment systems end-to-end — from ISO 20022 file validation (PAIN and CAMT) to live penny tests with banks and customers.

 - Lead technical bank integrations and payment projects, covering scoping, architecture, and delivery across domestic and international payments.

 - Investigate and resolve payment or statement issues using observability tools, logs, and database queries.

 - Improve reliability and scalability by streamlining processes, automating playbooks, and building maintainable backend infrastructure.

Your qualifications:

 - 2–3 years of experience building backend systems in payments, fintech, or financial services — ideally with H2H, SFTP, or ISO 20022 integrations.

 - Strong backend development skills in Kotlin, Java, Go, or Python, and solid understanding of databases (Postgres or similar).

 - Hands-on experience with payment file formats (PAIN and CAMT) and familiarity with SEPA, ACH, Wires, Faster Payments, CHAPS, and Bacs.

 - Clear communicator who can collaborate effectively with banks, customers, and teammates across time zones.

 - Structured, detail-oriented problem solver who takes ownership and continuously looks for process improvements.

 - Practical experience using AI or emerging technology tools to improve productivity, insight, or decision-making, with the ability to apply first-principles thinking to new problems.
   

Bonus points:

 - Direct experience integrating with card networks, payment gateways, or banking partners.

 - Built or scaled payment services in a high-growth startup.

 - Familiarity with AWS infrastructure, Docker, or Kubernetes.
   
   

Our Tech Stack:

 - Hosted on AWS

 - Postgres DB

 - Kotlin Monolith
   
   

Tips to stand out

 - Show us who you are through your applicatio

source_jobs.title: Product Manager
source_jobs.description:
Retire the ERP.

Light exists to replace factory-era ERPs with software that feels alive. Our Smart Financial Platform gives modern, global companies superpowers—automated accounting, real-time reporting, and financial flows that move at the speed of the business.

We build with our customers, ship fast, and obsess over craft. In a short time, Light has gone from idea to the operating core for leading companies like Lovable, Legora, and Keyshot. People don’t just use Light—they enjoy it.

We’re an early team defining a new software category. Think engineers who love debits and credits, designers who care about reconciliation states, and operators who treat finance as a product. If you’re excited to modernize how the world runs money—one workflow at a time—you’re in the right place.

Backed by world-class investors and advised by industry titans, we’re building category-defining products with the freedom to ship ambitiously and own outcomes.
Come help us make Light the global default for next-gen finance.



The Product Manager role

• Owning the delivery of product strategy and execution for a core area of Light from 0→1 to scale.

• Turning complex financial workflows into intuitive, AI-powered experiences.

• Partnering with Engineering, Design and Data to ship beautiful, high-impact products fast.

• Translating ambiguity into structure: defining clear priorities, metrics, and direction in alignment with engineering and go-to-market teams (Customer Success & Sales)

• Working directly with CFOs, controllers, and finance teams to uncover users insights and shape new automation.

• Making crisp, high-quality decisions to deliver promptly and effectively: balancing velocity, precision, and impact.



You’ll thrive here if you…

• You move fast, think deeply, and take ownership.

• You have a founder’s mindset — equally comfortable with spreadsheets, sketches, or specs.

• You thrive in structured chaos and get energy from solving hard problems.

• You want to build a category-defining product with people who expect excellence.



Your qualifications:

 - Multi-disciplinary experience broadly in product development: either product, engineering or design in a high-growth SaaS and AI startup companies

 - Ability to translate product vision and customer requirements into supportive guidance & direction for engineering teams

 - Influential experience in stakeholder engagement and communication at leadership and delivery team level

 - Practical experience using AI or emerging technology tools to improve productivity, insight, or decision-making, with the ability to apply first-principles thinking to new problems




Your traits

 - Exceptionally structured thinking - you cut through complexity fast

 - Strong analytical instincts; you think in systems and numbers

 - Clear communication and crisp written thinking

 - Bias for action: you’d rather test than theorise

 - Curiosity about how finance, automation, and AI intersect




Bonus points

 - Experience in B2B fintech, accounting, or payments

 - Exposure to automation, data-heavy products, or ML-powered workflows

 - Working cross-functionally in small, high-output teams

 - Technical fluency — you can talk APIs and workflows with engineers

 - Operating or founding at an early-stage startup

 - Management consulting (MBB or equivalent)
   
   

What success looks like in your first 6–12 months

🧭 A sharp, insight-driven roadmap aligned with Light’s mission.

🚀 Key features shipped that simplify and speed up finance operations.

📊 Metrics-driven decision-making embedded in your product area.

🤝 Engineering and Design teams love working with you because things move.

💡 You’re known as a force multiplier — adding clarity, speed, and structure everywhere you go.


Tips to stand out

 - Show how you’ve created clarity or structure from scratch.

 - Share examples of real outcomes — not just shipped features.

 - Walk us through your decision frameworks and tradeoffs.

 - Quantif

source_jobs.title: Implementation Manager
source_jobs.description:
Retire the ERP.

Light exists to replace factory-era ERPs with software that feels alive. Our Smart Financial Platform gives modern, global companies superpowers - automated accounting, real-time reporting, and financial flows that move at the speed of the business.



We build with our customers, ship fast, and obsess over craft. In a short time, Light has gone from idea to the operating core for leading companies like Lovable, Legora, and Keyshot. People don’t just use Light - they enjoy it.



We’re an early team defining a new software category. Think engineers who love debits and credits, designers who care about reconciliation states, and operators who treat finance as a product. If you’re excited to modernise how the world runs money - one workflow at a time - you’re in the right place.



Backed by world-class investors and advised by industry titans, we’re building category-defining products with the freedom to ship ambitiously and own outcomes. Come help us make Light the global default for next-gen finance.



About the Role

You'll own several concurrent implementations end to end — from contract signature to go-live. The plan, the customer relationship, the scope, the timeline, the outcome. You hand over to Customer Success once the customer is live.



Your customers are Financial Controllers, Finance Directors and CFOs at fast-growing multinational SaaS and tech companies. Senior, technical, time-poor. They expect you to keep up on multi-entity accounting, consolidation, intercompany, multi-currency and statutory reporting — and to tell them straight when they're asking for something that won't work.



No two implementations look the same. Every customer brings problems that don't have a textbook answer — a consolidation edge case, a migration shape the playbook doesn't cover, a process that needs rebuilding before it can be automated. You'll work the problem, ship the answer, and move on to the next one.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Own several concurrent implementations end to end, from kickoff to go-live

 - Run the customer relationship through implementation — Controllers, Finance Directors, CFOs

 - Translate finance requirements into platform design — chart of accounts, entity structure, consolidation logic, reporting

 - Lead data migration — chart of accounts mapping, opening balances, historical data, cutover

 - Hold scope through the project — keep delivery aligned to what was sold, surface and reset drift early

 - Push back on requests that won't work, and bring the customer with you

 - Spot projects going off-track before the customer does, and intervene

 - Train and enable customer finance teams to run on Light from day one of go-live

 - Contribute to the playbooks, templates, and patterns the team builds over time




EXPERIENCE & QUALIFICATIONS

 - Degree in finance, accounting, business, or a related field

 - 5+ years in finance transformation, ERP implementation, or finance advisory — Big 4, a finance-focused boutique, or a vendor-side professional services team (NetSuite, Sage Intacct, Workday Financials)

 - You are fluent in accounting and can hold your own with a Controller on multi-entity consolidation, intercompany eliminations, accrual accounting, statutory vs management reporting. Qualified accountant is a plus, not a requirement

 - You bring structure to messy projects — clear plans, sharp prioritisation, and the judgement to know which details matter and which don't.

 - You excel at building trust with C-suite executives and finance leadership teams through clear communication and consistent delivery.

 - You are comfortable working cross-functionally — you have delivered through Product, Engineering, Sales, and customer teams without formal authority over any of them

 - You diagnose implementation issues quickly and deliver practical solutions that balance customer needs with technical feasibility.

 - You have a real implementation with your name on the go-live — vendor-si

source_jobs.title: Integration Engineer (CRM/HRIS)
source_jobs.description:
Retire the ERP.

Light exists to replace factory-era ERPs with software that feels alive. Our Smart Financial Platform gives modern, global companies superpowers—automated accounting, real-time reporting, and financial flows that move at the speed of the business.

We build with our customers, ship fast, and obsess over craft. In a short time, Light has gone from idea to the operating core for leading companies like Lovable, Legora, and Keyshot. People don’t just use Light—they enjoy it.

We’re an early team defining a new software category. Think engineers who love debits and credits, designers who care about reconciliation states, and operators who treat finance as a product. If you’re excited to modernize how the world runs money—one workflow at a time—you’re in the right place.

Backed by world-class investors and advised by industry titans, we’re building category-defining products with the freedom to ship ambitiously and own outcomes.
Come help us make Light the global default for next-gen finance.



The Role

Light's customers need their financial platform connected to their CRM (HubSpot, Salesforce, Pipedrive) and HRM systems (BambooHR, Personio, Deel, etc.). This role owns all CRM/HRM integration work - building connectors, maintaining existing ones, and being the go-to person when customers need their people/sales data flowing into Light.
You'll work closely with Customer Delivery on implementations and the Solutions Architecture group on connector design patterns. This isn't a support role - you're an engineer building and improving how Light connects to these systems.



What You'll Do

 - Build and maintain integrations with major CRM platforms (Salesforce, HubSpot, Pipedrive) and HRM systems (BambooHR, Personio, Deel, Workday)

 - Own the integration patterns and shared libraries for CRM/HRM connectors

 - Work directly with customers during onboarding to configure and troubleshoot their integrations

 - Document APIs and integration approaches so the team can scale knowledge

 - Triage and resolve integration-related production issues

 - Partner with Product to prioritise which connectors to build next based on customer demand



Your Qualifications:

 - Strong backend engineering (Kotlin preferred, Java/JVM acceptable)

 - Experience building and maintaining third-party API integrations

 - Comfortable with OAuth flows, webhooks, rate limiting, and the messiness of real-world APIs

 - PostgreSQL knowledge

 - Familiarity with at least one major CRM or HRM platform's API

 - Pragmatic approach - these integrations need to work reliably, not win architecture awards

 - Practical experience using AI or emerging technology tools to improve productivity, insight, or decision-making, with the ability to apply first-principles thinking to new problems.
   
   

Nice to Have

 - Experience with financial data or ERP integrations

 - Previous work in a B2B SaaS environment

 - Exposure to AWS (EKS, Lambda, RDS)
   
   

Why This Role Exists
CRM/HRM integrations are a growing part of our customer onboarding and ongoing support load. Having a dedicated specialist means faster implementations, better connector quality, and a single point of accountability. You'll have real ownership and direct customer impact.



The good stuff

In addition to being part of a great team and working in a really fun and innovative environment, we offer:

💸 A competitive salary + potential stock options
🏝 25 days of annual leave + public holidays in the country where you are based.
🥳 Regular socials and company off-sites
🚀 A huge opportunity for career growth as you'll help shape a market-defining product.



The famous last words

Working at Light means you're embarking on an exciting journey to reshape the future of multinational financial operations. Our cheat-code is that we've built something like this before - and are now doing it again, only better. Our goal is to make global finance effortless and empower companies to make informed

source_jobs.title: Global Compliance Manager
source_jobs.description:
Retire the ERP.

Light exists to replace factory-era ERPs with software that feels alive. Our Smart Financial Platform gives modern, global companies superpowers—automated accounting, real-time reporting, and financial flows that move at the speed of the business.

We build with our customers, ship fast, and obsess over craft. In a short time, Light has gone from idea to the operating core for leading companies like Lovable, Legora, and Keyshot. People don’t just use Light—they enjoy it.

We’re an early team defining a new software category. Think engineers who love debits and credits, designers who care about reconciliation states, and operators who treat finance as a product. If you’re excited to modernize how the world runs money—one workflow at a time—you’re in the right place.

Backed by world-class investors and advised by industry titans, we’re building category-defining products with the freedom to ship ambitiously and own outcomes.
Come help us make Light the global default for next-gen finance.




THE GLOBAL COMPLIANCE MANAGER ROLE

You’ll build Light’s compliance function from the ground up. There’s no playbook yet. You’ll work across engineering, legal, and customer-facing teams to turn ambiguous requirements into a working system - owning the judgment calls and building the processes that make compliance clear, defensible, and scalable as we grow.

As fintech organisation in AI age our customers rely on us to keep their data safe, you’ll be the single point of accountability for our SOC 1, SOC 2, and PCI programmes - designing how compliance works here, not maintaining a system that already exists.

What you'll build

 - Own compliance decisions and tradeoffs across the business — there's no existing framework, you're writing it

 - Interpret compliance frameworks and define the path forward for real situations as they come up

 - Run SOC 1, SOC 2 (Type I & II), and PCI DSS end to end

 - Build the audit calendar, evidence process, and auditor relationships

 - Track findings and drive remediation to completion

 - Build and maintain risk registers, exceptions, and compensating controls

 - Build our customer security questionnaire and vendor due diligence process

 - Partner with Finance, Engineering, and Product to embed compliance into how features get built

How you fit in

You think like a builder, not a caretaker — energised by starting from a blank page rather than maintaining what already exists. You make decisive calls on ambiguous problems rather than waiting for someone else to define the answer, and you care more about controls that work in practice than perfect documentation. Organised, persistent, calm under pressure, and able to bring technical and non-technical people together around a system you've designed.

Your qualifications

 - 8+ years in compliance, risk, or security, ideally including experience standing up a compliance function, not just running one

 - Demonstrated experience interpreting frameworks and building process from scratch

 - Hands-on ownership of SOC 1, SOC 2, and/or PCI DSS

 - Comfortable coordinating with engineering as a decision-maker, without doing the technical work yourself

 - Strong written and verbal communication skills

 - Comfortable using AI tools to build and scale compliance processes

Bonus points

 - Experience building a compliance function at an early-stage or high-growth company

 - Fintech, payments, or financial systems experience

 - Compliance tooling experience (Vanta, Drata, Secureframe)

 - Background in engineering, IT operations, or security



THE GOOD STUFF

In addition to being part of a great team and working in a really fun and innovative environment, we offer:

💸 Competitive salary + potential stock options
🏝 25 days of annual leave + public holidays
🥳 Regular socials and company Starsite off-sites
🚀 A huge opportunity for career growth as you will help shape a market-defining product



THE FAMOUS LAST WORDS

Working at Light means you a

source_jobs.title: AI Strategy & Operations Associate
source_jobs.description:

We build with our customers, ship fast, and obsess over craft. In a short time, Light has gone from idea to the operating core for leading companies like Lovable, Legora, and Keyshot. People don’t just use Light—they enjoy it.

We’re an early team defining a new software category. Think engineers who love debits and credits, designers who care about reconciliation states, and operators who treat finance as a product. If you’re excited to modernize how the world runs money—one workflow at a time—you’re in the right place.

Backed by world-class investors and advised by industry titans, we’re building category-defining products with the freedom to ship ambitiously and own outcomes.
Come help us make Light the global default for next-gen finance.





THE ROLE

This is a foundational hire on Light's Finance & Operations team. You will work directly with the Head of Finance & Operations across Finance, Legal & Compliance, People Operations, and General Operations providing execution-layer support across all of them.

The timing matters. Light is at the stage where the foundational processes are being built for the first time. Whoever is in this role will have a hand in designing how things work, not just running what already exists. That is a rare thing to be part of early in a career, and the experience compounds fast.

This is not a narrow role. On any given week you might be creating a new FinOps flow for vendor contracts tracking, helping set up a new equity structure or onboarding process, or mapping a compliance deadline across four jurisdictions. The common thread is precision, ownership, and the ability to move fast without losing detail.

We are deliberately looking for someone early in their career. Not as a cost decision, as a capability decision. The best person for this role thinks natively in modern tools and AI workflows, not in the processes those tools are replacing. If you have spent years optimising legacy ways of working, this is probably not the right fit. If you instinctively reach for automation before repetition, it might be exactly right.

This role can also be offered as a 3 - 6 month internship with an option to convert to a full-time role.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Work across finance operations, legal & compliance, people and general operations as an execution layer directly supporting the Head of Finance & Operations

 - Support finance operations and processes: vendor and customer tracking, finance flows across tools etc.

 - Support people operations from onboarding, HR data, contracting etc.

 - Manage the company's legal document library, contract administration, and entity-level filing obligations, ensuring nothing falls through the cracks

 - Identify where recurring manual work can be automated or standardised, and build the AI-based solution rather than waiting for someone else to do so
   
   


WHAT WE ARE LOOKING FOR

 - 1-3 years of experience in a fast-paced environment: MBB consulting, investment banking, a high-growth startup, or a similarly demanding setting

 - Degree in business, economics, finance, accounting, or a related field with demonstrable academic strength

 - Solid accounting fundamentals and experience in financial modelling preferred - you understand how the numbers work and can build structured analysis in Excel

 - Exceptional attention to detail and genuine ownership, tasks get closed, not handed back

 - Genuinely AI-native, you use AI tools daily, think in agents and automation first, and find manual repetitive workflows frustrating rather than comfortable. This is a hard filter, not a nice-to-have

 - A demonstrated interest in building, process improvements, internal tools, or workflow automation

source_jobs.title: People Partner
source_jobs.description:
Note: this role is 5 days a week onsite




ABOUT LIGHT

Light is the AI-native finance platform built for hypergrowth. Founded in 2022, we've reimagined finance software from the ground up—processing hundreds of millions of records in under a second and automating multi-entity accounting across global jurisdictions.

We're trusted by companies like Lovable and Legora, and backed by world-class investors including Balderton Capital, Atomico, Cherry Ventures, and Seedcamp. In summer 2025, we raised $30M in Series A funding, bringing total funding to $43M.

While traditional ERPs bolt AI onto legacy systems, Light was built AI-first—from database design to user experience. The result: finance teams reduce operational workload by 84% and expand into new countries in days, not months.

We’re expanding globally across London, Copenhagen, and soon New York. Join us as we build the finance platform that hypergrowth companies have been waiting for.




THE ROLE

We’re hiring a People Partner to own and evolve critical People systems & processes as Light continues through rapid scaling and growing across teams, geographies and complexity.

This is a high ownership IC role, focused on continuous improvement and enabling a high performance organisation.

Over the last 6 months we’ve gone from 40 → 90 and we are looking to make an additional 30+ hires this year.

You will report directly to the Head of Finance & Core Ops, and work closely with the wider leadership team, acting as a key partner on people related decisions, while also building and executing within all pillars of HR




WHAT YOU WILL BE DOING



PERFORMANCE MANAGEMENT & FEEDBACK SYSTEMS

 - Own the design, execution, and iteration of Light’s performance review and feedback cycles

 - Ensure performance processes are rigorous, consistent, and drive high-performance behaviours

 - Work with leadership to maintain clear expectations, accountability, and promotion decisions


COMPENSATION & PROGRESSION FRAMEWORKS

 - Own compensation review cycles, benchmarking, and internal alignment processes

 - Build, maintain and evolve role levels and progression frameworks across the company

 - Ensure compensation and promotion decisions are structured, fair, and consistently applied


ORG DESIGN & LEADERSHIP PARTNERING

 - Partner with leadership on org structure, scaling decisions, and team effectiveness

 - Advise on organisational changes during growth, ensuring clarity and operational effectiveness

 - Identify and resolve structural bottlenecks as the company scales


HIRING & TALENT PARTNERING

 - Partner with Finance and leadership on workforce planning and headcount allocation across teams and geographies

 - Support definition and structuring of roles to ensure clarity of scope, level, and organisational fit

 - Provide input into hiring prioritisation and sequencing as the company scales


PEOPLE OPERATIONS & SYSTEMS

 - Own core People processes including onboarding, offboarding, and employee lifecycle moments

 - Improve HR systems and tooling (e.g. HiBob) to ensure scalability and accuracy

 - Build operational clarity across People processes as the company grows


CULTURE & WAYS OF WORKING

 - Shape and reinforce effective ways of working as Light scales

 - Identify cultural and organisational friction points and drive improvements with leadership

 - Strengthen communication rhythms, alignment, and operational cadence across teams


COMPANY WORKPLACE & EVENTS

 - Own the day-to-day workplace experience across Light’s London office, ensuring it is functional, well-run, and aligned with company culture

 - Own planning and execution of company offsites and key team events

 - Design experiences that strengthen alignment, trust, and cohesion across global teams
   
   


YOUR QUALIFICATIONS

 - 5+ years experience in a People Partner, People Manager or HRBP role in a high-growth environment

 - Ownership of people systems (performance, compensation, hiring, onboarding)

 -

source_jobs.title: Integration Engineer
source_jobs.description:
RETIRE THE ERP.


Light exists to replace factory-era ERPs with software that feels alive. Our Smart Financial Platform gives modern, global companies superpowers—automated accounting, real-time reporting, and financial flows that move at the speed of the business.

We build with our customers, ship fast, and obsess over craft. In a short time, Light has gone from idea to the operating core for leading companies like Lovable, Legora, and Keyshot. People don't just use Light—they enjoy it.

We're an early team defining a new software category. Think engineers who love debits and credits, designers who care about reconciliation states, and operators who treat finance as a product. If you're excited to modernize how the world runs money—one workflow at a time—you're in the right place.

Backed by world-class investors and advised by industry titans, we're building category-defining products with the freedom to ship ambitiously and own outcomes. Come help us make Light the global default for next-gen finance.




THIS INTEGRATIONS ENGINEER ROLE

Light is only as powerful as the systems it connects to. Money doesn't move because we say so—it moves because we speak the language of the bank, the e-invoicing network, and the planning tool on the other side, correctly, every time, for every customer entity. This role owns that layer: the interaction surface between Light and the rest of the financial world.

You'll build and operate the integrations that let Light plug into multi-tenant banking APIs (J.P. Morgan, HSBC, and peers), global e-invoicing networks, and tax platforms and more. This is backend-heavy, data-integrity-critical work where a dropped message or a silent reconciliation mismatch is not an acceptable outcome. You'll design the abstractions that turn a dozen incompatible partner APIs into one coherent internal model, and you'll keep those abstractions honest as partners change their specs underneath you.



What you'll work on:

 - Bank connectivity. Build against corporate banking and payments APIs—account information, payment initiation, status reporting, FX—across providers like J.P. Morgan Payments and HSBC. Handle the realities of bank integrations: certificate-based and mTLS auth, OAuth flows, ISO 20022 (pain/pacs/camt) and legacy MT formats, sandbox-to-production parity, and rate limits that assume you're a bank, not a startup.

 - Multi-tenancy done right. Our customers are many legal entities operating in many countries. You'll design integrations where credentials, configuration, and data are isolated per tenant, where onboarding a new entity is a workflow rather than a code change, and where one customer's failure never leaks into another's.

 - Global e-invoicing. Connect Light to e-invoicing networks and country-specific mandates (Peppol and the growing set of national clearance and reporting regimes). Map between formats, manage compliance-driven edge cases, and keep delivery auditable.

 - Tax platforms for statutory filing, US sales tax and much more.

 - Reliability and reconciliation. Idempotency, retries, webhook and polling strategies, dead-letter handling, and end-to-end reconciliation so that what we think happened and what actually happened always agree.
   
   


YOUR QUALIFICATIONS

4-6 years of experience ideally, plus…

You build things. Not prototypes, not demos. Working software in production, doing something real for real users. This is the non-negotiable.

You've built integrations against unforgiving external systems. APIs you don't control, that fail in ways the docs don't mention, where correctness is contractual. You know how to defend a system against a partner that returns a 200 with an error in the body.

You know banking and payments APIs. Direct, hands-on experience with corporate banking, treasury, or payments APIs is what sets the strongest candidates apart. Experience integrating J.P. Morgan or HSBC specifically—their developer platforms, auth models, and payment and reporting

source_jobs.title: Marketing Manager (DACH)
source_jobs.description:
THE FINANCE FUNCTION IS BROKEN.

Finance teams at fast-growing, multi-entity companies are outgrowing their single-entity ledgers and facing a familiar crossroads: bolt on more tools, or go full corporate ERP. Neither option is good. The Frankenstack breaks. The ERP weighs you down with implementation fees and consultants. There has to be a better, more AI native way.

There is. It's Light.

Light is building an AI-native financial platform for modern companies. Fast to implement, intuitive to use, and designed to get smarter over time. We call it organic software.

Companies like Legora, Lovable, and Fuse Energy already run on Light. Following our $30M Series A last year, we're building the team that will make Light the default finance platform for the next generation of businesses.

 
 


WHY DACH, WHY NOW.

To win Europe, you have to win DACH. We know this. That's why we're making our first dedicated hire for the region and treating it as a must-win market. You won't be a satellite of a global team running templated playbooks. You'll be a builder. THe person who decides what Light looks and feels like in Germany, Austria, and Switzerland.

 
 


WHAT YOU'LL BE DOING.

 - Building Light's presence in DACH from the ground up, shaping our GTM strategy, and becoming one of the defining faces of the region. If you'd rather set the direction than follow it, this is your gig.

 - Owning the full DACH events calendar. Finhack hackathons, third-party sponsorships, roundtables. From concept to follow-up, you run it.

 - Managing the regional budget, knowing where to spend and where to hold back.

 - Working with our brand team on everything from regional messaging and localisation to experiential campaigns that cut through in a market full of noise.

 - Bringing on-the-ground market data back to your VP Marketing and Growth Lead to sharpen paid activity, targeting, and messaging across the region.

 - Driving regional pipeline, working across events, campaigns, and community to build a consistent flow of qualified opportunities for the sales team.

 - Partnering with RevOps to keep pipeline clean, prospects moving, and nothing falling through the cracks between marketing and sales.

 - Enabling the sales team with the collateral, messaging, and market context they need to win in DACH.

 - Building community, not just pipeline. You'll build relationships with press, analysts, and partners, but the bigger goal is an ecosystem of likeminded changemakers around a product that's transforming the finance function.

 
 


WHAT WE THINK YOU'LL NEED.*

 - Meaningful experience in field or regional marketing at a B2B SaaS company, ideally one that was building out international presence from a non-DACH HQ.

 - Native-level German fluency. You can write the copy, run the room, and build the relationships.

 - A real track record running field marketing and events from start to finish..

 - The ability to work across Sales, RevOps, and brand without losing momentum or creating friction. You know how to get things done in a cross-functional team.

 - Genuine enthusiasm for AI tooling, not just comfort with it. We're an AI-native company and use AI across everything we build and do. Just as Light helps finance teams do their best work, we expect our people to use AI to do theirs.

 - To be based in Copenhagen or London, with frequent travel to the region.

*But really, what do we know? If the crux of the role sounds right but you don't tick every box, reach out anyway and make your case.

 
 

The good stuff.

🏢 In-office culture, five days a week, with a high talent-density team.
💸 Competitive salary.
🏝 25 days annual leave plus public holidays in your country of employment.
🚀 The chance to shape a category from the inside, not just market around it.
🌍 Regular travel to Copenhagen, London, New York, and wherever the action is.
🥳 Company off-sites twice a year. The last one was in Italy.

 
 

Light is an equal opportunity employer.

source_jobs.title: Engineering Manager
source_jobs.description:
Retire the ERP.

Light exists to replace factory-era ERPs with software that feels alive. Our Smart Financial Platform gives modern, global companies superpowers—automated accounting, real-time reporting, and financial flows that move at the speed of the business.

We build with our customers, ship fast, and obsess over craft. In a short time, Light has gone from idea to the operating core for leading companies like Lovable, Legora, and Keyshot. People don’t just use Light—they enjoy it.

We’re an early team defining a new software category. Think engineers who love debits and credits, designers who care about reconciliation states, and operators who treat finance as a product. If you’re excited to modernize how the world runs money—one workflow at a time—you’re in the right place.

Backed by world-class investors and advised by industry titans, we’re building category-defining products with the freedom to ship ambitiously and own outcomes.
Come help us make Light the global default for next-gen finance.



This Engineering Manager role

You'll lead one of our engineering teams: five to seven engineers building a core part of the Smart Financial Platform. You own the team's health, its standards, and its delivery. This is a hands-on leadership role. You stay close enough to the code to earn your engineers' trust and make real technical calls, but your team's output comes from them, not from you holding the keyboard.

You'll report to the Head of Engineering and work alongside a Product Manager who owns what gets built and why. Your job is to build a team that ships it well.



How you fit into the team

We run small, autonomous teams with a clear split: Product owns the commitment, engineering owns the delivery. You're the person who turns a product commitment into something your team can actually ship, on a sensible timeline, at a quality bar you set and hold. You grow the people on your team, you keep the work humane, and you're the first to know when something's drifting, never the last.



What you'll own

 - Own the complete system of your domain end-to-end, ensuring world-class reliability, correctness, and scalability across every entity and jurisdiction Light serves.

 - Design and build the infrastructure and internal tooling that lets the rest of the org move faster on [domain].

 - Implement market-specific compliance and regulatory requirements (VAT, e-invoicing, local GAAP) to accelerate Light's European expansion.

 - Break down ambiguous product commitments into shippable work, set realistic timelines, and renegotiate early when reality changes.

 - Recruit, build, and lead a high-performing engineering team — own the funnel for open roles and a new joiner's first 90 days to productive.

 - Hands-on ownership of key features in your domain. You ship code, not just review it.

 - Run people growth and performance with the same rigor you apply to delivery: named growth focus per engineer, drift caught early, nothing a surprise at review time.
   
   

Basic qualifications

 - Exceptional software engineering background with deep experience building large-scale, high-stakes systems (financial, transactional, or accounting platforms preferred).

 - Proven ability to lead technical initiatives and directly manage engineering teams.

 - Strong track record delivering complex backend systems in production.

 - Passion for financial infrastructure and building products companies actually rely on.
   
   

Preferred skills and experience

 - 5+ years on large-scale systems, ideally in fintech, accounting, or multi-entity financial operations.

 - Experience leading engineering in a fast-paced, high-growth environment.

 - Expertise in distributed systems, multi-book ledger design, or compliance/regulatory infrastructure.

 - Proficiency with Kotlin, React, or comfort getting fluent fast.



The good stuff

In addition to being part of a great team and working in a really fun and innovative environment, we offer:

💸 A com

source_jobs.title: Account Manager
source_jobs.description:
Retire the ERP

Light exists to replace factory-era ERPs with software that feels alive. Our Smart Financial Platform gives modern, global companies superpowers - automated accounting, real-time reporting, and financial flows that move at the speed of the business.

We build with our customers, ship fast, and obsess over craft. In a short time, Light has gone from idea to the operating core for leading companies like Lovable, Legora, and Fuse Energy.

We're an early team defining a new software category. Think engineers who love debits and credits, designers who care about reconciliation states, and operators who treat finance as a product. If you're excited to modernise how companies run money, you're in the right place.

Backed by world-class investors including Balderton and Atomico and advised by industry titans, we're building category-defining products with the freedom to ship ambitiously and own outcomes. Come help us make Light the global default for next-gen finance.



About the Role

You'll sit at the heart of our Customer function and work with our most important customers. You’ll make sure they understand the value Light brings them and drive renewals and upsell.

Your customers are Financial Controllers, Finance Directors and CFOs at fast-growing multinational SaaS and tech companies. Senior, technical, time-poor. They expect you to keep up, to tell them straight when something won't work, and to make their team the hero.

We’re a young company, and we’re just starting to renew contracts. In parallel, we’re deploying Agentic Customer Engineers who help our customers make the most of Light. While they re-design customer workflows and deploy agents, you’ll own the stakeholders, the adoption, and the commercial in this motion. There's no set playbook yet. You'll write it, and we'll help.



Key Responsibilities

 - Own existing customer relationships and drive adoption, renewal and upsell

 - Ensure that Controllers, Finance Directors, and CFOs see the value Light brings

 - Make finance the hero - help them do their best work and get recognition for it

 - Partner two-in-a-box with Agentic Customer Engineers to help customers make the most of Light and our suite of AI agents

 - Spot an engagement going sideways before the customer does, and fix it

 - Feed what you learn back into the playbook the team is building



Experience & Qualifications

 - 5+ years owning customers in account management, customer success, consulting, or a commercial delivery role - with outcomes you can point to

 - You hold your own with a CFO. You can talk multi-entity finance, ROI, and adoption without flinching, and you know when to push back

 - You've driven adoption and expansion, not just kept accounts warm and sent QBRs

 - You bring structure to messy, ambiguous engagements - clear plans, sharp prioritisation, and the judgement to know which details matter

 - You've owned accounts and driven outcomes

 - You use AI tools daily and have specific opinions on where they help and where they don't

 - Fluent English; additional European languages a plus

 - Bonus #1: Finance or accounting background

 - Bonus #2: Experience in a forward-deployed, solutions-led, or professional-services model focused on renewals and upsell, ideally in the context of AI
   
   

You'll probably struggle here if

 - You need a big support team, a mature playbook, and a manager on call to operate - this is a founding role with limited scaffolding

 - You can't give a concrete example of telling a senior customer "no" and making it stick

 - You love the status quo - this job is about change more than maintenance

 - You've used ChatGPT once and weren't impressed - we go all in on AI at Light
   
   

The good stuff

In addition to being part of a great team and working in a really fun and innovative environment, we offer:

💸 A competitive salary + potential stock options

🏝 Generous package of paid annual leave + public holidays

🥳 Regular socials and c

source_jobs.title: Agentic Customer Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Retire the ERP

Light exists to replace factory-era ERPs with software that feels alive. Our Smart Financial Platform gives modern, global companies superpowers - automated accounting, real-time reporting, and financial flows that move at the speed of the business.

We build with our customers, ship fast, and obsess over craft. In a short time, Light has gone from idea to the operating core for leading companies like Lovable, Legora, and Fuse Energy.

We're an early team defining a new software category. Think engineers who love debits and credits, designers who care about reconciliation states, and operators who treat finance as a product. If you're excited to modernise how companies run money, you're in the right place.

Backed by world-class investors including Balderton and Atomico and advised by industry titans, we're building category-defining products with the freedom to ship ambitiously and own outcomes. Come help us make Light the global default for next-gen finance.



About the Role

You'll embed inside our most important customers and rebuild how their finance function works. You’ll map how the team really works, redesign the workflow, wire the integrations, and ship the agents, all to make sure they work in AI-native ways and are using Light to its full potential.

Your customers are Financial Controllers, Finance Directors and CFOs at fast-growing multinational SaaS and tech companies. Senior, technical, time-poor. You'll sit with them, diagnose the real problem, build the answer - then train them to run it without you.

This is a founding role in a new motion. Part process designer, part builder and deployer. You partner with an Account Manager who owns the stakeholders and the commercial; you own the process re-design and agent deployment. No two customers look the same, and there's no set playbook yet. You'll write it, and we’ll help.



Key Responsibilities

 - Embed with a customer's finance team, map and redesign their workflows AI-first

 - Wire the integrations - pull data from ERPs, banks, and billing into Light

 - Configure, ship, and supervise AI agents in production until the customer trusts them

 - Hand back a workflow the customer runs themselves, and train their team from go-live

 - Diagnose the edge cases the playbook doesn't cover, and ship the answer

 - Measure & prove the impact against a week-0 baseline

 - Partner two-in-a-box with your Account Manager, and pull in platform and AI engineering when you need them

 - Feed repeatable patterns to the Product roadmap
   
   

Experience & Qualifications

 - 3+ years close to how finance teams actually work - accounting, process design, consulting, or technical customer success

 - You build & tinker. Vibe coding, data pipelines, and LLM agents are tools you reach for, and you ship working software, not slideware

 - You're fluent enough in finance to hold your own with a Controller on multi-entity, consolidation, and reporting - and to know when a request won't work

 - You bring structure to messy problems and move fast: diagnose, prototype, harden, ship

 - You're comfortable in front of a CFO and next to an engineer, and you've delivered without formal authority over anyone

 - You use AI tools daily and have specific opinions on where they help in delivery and where they don't

 - Fluent English; additional European languages a plus

 - Bonus #1: Solutions or integration engineering background; experience mapping and redesigning finance processes and with AI agents in production (evals, guardrails, …)

 - Bonus #2: A finance / accounting qualification
   
   

You'll probably struggle here if

 - You need a spec handed to you - here you find the problem, then build the answer

 - You've only worked on internal products, never sat in front of a customer

 - You think "redesign the process" means "automate what exists". The job is to rethink it from first principles and inject AI where it makes sense

 - You want a big platform team to lean

notes: High-tech startup with strong engineering focus, AI-native financial platform
